summary: Tested the P0 logout fix for PortHop. The auth guard (RootLayoutNav in _layout.tsx) is correctly implemented and WORKS. However, Alert.alert in react-native-web v0.21.0 is a no-op (static alert() {}), so the full logout flow cannot be completed via the web preview - the confirmation dialog never appears when clicking Logout. Testing confirmed the auth guard logic is correct via simulation: removing the token from localStorage and reloading correctly redirects to the phone login screen. On actual iOS/Android devices, Alert.alert works natively, so the full logout flow should work correctly.

ui_bugs.issue: Alert.alert in react-native-web v0.21.0 is implemented as a no-op (static alert() {}). The logout confirmation dialog never appears in the web preview. The logout flow is completely broken in web. On mobile device it should work since native Alert.alert works.
ui_bugs.priority: MEDIUM (web preview only, mobile should work)

react_native_issues.issue: react-native-web does not implement Alert.alert (it's a no-op). The logout confirmation flow is broken in web preview. Consider using a custom Modal-based confirmation dialog or Platform.select() to use window.confirm on web.

action_items: Replace Alert.alert in profile screens with a cross-platform solution: either a custom React Native Modal confirmation dialog, or use Platform.OS check to use window.confirm on web and Alert.alert on native. This will fix the web preview logout.
The auth guard (RootLayoutNav useEffect in _layout.tsx) is confirmed working correctly - no action needed there.
Optionally: verify logout works on actual Expo Go mobile app since Alert.alert is native there.
critical_code_review_comments: Alert.alert is a known no-op in react-native-web. Profile screens should use Platform-aware confirmation: import {Platform} from 'react-native' and use Platform.select or conditional logic to show a Modal on web vs Alert.alert on native.
The auth guard pattern in _layout.tsx (RootLayoutNav watching user/loading/segments) is correctly implemented following the canonical Expo Router auth guard pattern.

success_rate.frontend: 50% - Auth guard works correctly, but Alert.alert is broken in web preventing full logout flow test

context_for_next_testing_agent: Alert.alert is a no-op in react-native-web. The auth guard IS fixed and working. To fully test logout in web preview, the Alert.alert needs to be replaced with a Platform-aware solution. The fix can be tested by: 1) checking that clicking Logout shows a confirmation, 2) confirming redirects to /phone screen.
rca_of_issue: Root Cause: react-native-web v0.21.0 implements Alert as 'static alert() {}' (complete no-op). When the Logout button is clicked, handleLogout() calls Alert.alert() which silently does nothing - no dialog appears, no callback is invoked, logout() is never called. The auth guard fix in _layout.tsx is correct and would work if logout() is successfully called. Fix: Replace Alert.alert with Platform.OS === 'web' ? window.confirm(...) : Alert.alert(...) pattern, OR use a custom Modal-based confirmation dialog.
